acpi.description: Whether to enable ACPI (defaults to `true`).

amdSev.description: Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) features by AMD CPUs.

bios.description: The BIOS implementation (defaults to `seabios`).

bootOrders.description: Specify a list of devices to boot from in the order they appear in the list.

deleteUnreferencedDisksOnDestroy.description: Whether to delete unreferenced disks on destroy (defaults to `true`)

description.description: The description.

efiDisk.description: The efi disk device (required if `bios` is set to `ovmf`)

hookScriptFileId.description: The identifier for a file containing a hook script (needs to be executable, e.g. by using the `proxmox_virtual_environment_file.file_mode` attribute).

hostpcis.description: A host PCI device mapping (multiple blocks supported).

hotplug.description: Selectively enable hotplug features. Use `0` to disable, `1` to enable all. Valid features: `disk`, `network`, `usb`, `memory`, `cpu`. Memory hotplug requires NUMA to be enabled. If not set, PVE defaults to `network,disk,usb`. When `disk` is included in the hotplug list, disk resizes on a running VM are applied live without a reboot. When `disk` is excluded, the provider will reboot the VM after resize (controlled by `rebootAfterUpdate`).

ipv4Addresses.description: The IPv4 addresses per network interface published by the QEMU agent (empty list when `agent.enabled` is `false`)

ipv6Addresses.description: The IPv6 addresses per network interface published by the QEMU agent (empty list when `agent.enabled` is `false`)

macAddresses.description:
The MAC addresses published by the QEMU agent with fallback
to the network device configuration, if the agent is disabled

machine.description: The VM machine type (defaults to `pc`).

memory.description: The memory configuration.

migrate.description: Migrate the VM on node change instead of re-creating it (defaults to `false`).

name.description: The virtual machine name. Must be a valid DNS name.

networkInterfaceNames.description: The network interface names published by the QEMU agent (empty list when `agent.enabled` is `false`)

nodeName.description:
The name of the node to assign the virtual machine
to.

onBoot.description: Specifies whether a VM will be started during system boot. (defaults to `true`)

poolId.description: The identifier for a pool to assign the virtual machine to.

protection.description: Sets the protection flag of the VM. This will disable the remove VM and remove disk operations (defaults to `false`).

purgeOnDestroy.description: Whether to purge the VM from backup configurations on destroy (defaults to `true`)

reboot.description: Reboot the VM after initial creation (defaults to `false`).

rebootAfterUpdate.description: Whether the provider may automatically reboot or power off the VM during update operations when required to apply changes. If `false`, updates that require taking the VM offline fail instead of being applied automatically. Changes that are applied successfully but still need a later manual reboot emit a warning instead (defaults to `true`).

rngs.description: The random number generator configuration. Can only be set by `root@pam.`

smbios.description: The SMBIOS (type1) settings for the VM.

started.description: Whether to start the virtual machine (defaults to `true`).

startup.description: Defines startup and shutdown behavior of the VM.

stopOnDestroy.description: Whether to stop rather than shutdown on VM destroy (defaults to `false`)

tabletDevice.description: Whether to enable the USB tablet device (defaults to `true`).

tags.description: A list of tags of the VM. This is only meta information ( defaults to `[]`). Note: Proxmox always sorts the VM tags. If the list in template is not sorted, then Proxmox will always report a difference on the resource. You may use the `ignoreChanges` lifecycle meta-argument to ignore changes to this attribute.

template.description: Whether the VM should be a template. Setting this from `false` to `true` converts an existing VM to a template in place. Converting a template back to a regular VM is not supported (defaults to `false`).

timeoutClone.description:
Timeout for cloning a VM in seconds (defaults to
1800).

timeoutCreate.description:
Timeout for creating a VM in seconds (defaults to
1800).

timeoutMigrate.description:
Timeout for migrating the VM (defaults to
1800).

timeoutMoveDisk.deprecationMessage: This field is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. An overall operation timeout (timeout_create / timeoutClone / timeout_migrate) is used instead.

timeoutReboot.description:
Timeout for rebooting a VM in seconds (defaults
to 1800).

timeoutShutdownVm.description:
Timeout for shutting down a VM in seconds (
defaults to 1800).

timeoutStartVm.description:
Timeout for starting a VM in seconds (defaults
to 1800).

timeoutStopVm.description:
Timeout for stopping a VM in seconds (defaults
to 300).

tpmState.description: The TPM state device. The VM must be stopped before adding, removing, or moving a TPM state device; the provider automatically handles the shutdown/start cycle. Changing `version` requires recreating the VM because Proxmox only supports setting the TPM version at creation time.

usbs.description: A host USB device mapping (multiple blocks supported).

watchdog.description: The watchdog configuration. Once enabled (by a guest action), the watchdog must be periodically polled by an agent inside the guest or else the watchdog will reset the guest (or execute the respective action specified).

usbs.description: A host USB device mapping (multiple blocks supported).

watchdog.description: The watchdog configuration. Once enabled (by a guest action), the watchdog must be periodically polled by an agent inside the guest or else the watchdog will reset the guest (or execute the respective action specified).

disable.description: Set this to `true` to disable this metric server. Defaults to `false`.

graphiteProto.description: Protocol to send graphite data. Choice is between `udp` | `tcp`. If not set, PVE default is `udp`.

influxApiPathPrefix.description: An API path prefix inserted between `<host>:<port>/` and `/api2/`. Can be useful if the InfluxDB service runs behind a reverse proxy.

influxBucket.description: The InfluxDB bucket/db. Only necessary when using the http v2 api.

influxDbProto.description: Protocol for InfluxDB. Choice is between `udp` | `http` | `https`. If not set, PVE default is `udp`.

influxMaxBodySize.description: InfluxDB max-body-size in bytes. Requests are batched up to this size. If not set, PVE default is `25000000`.

influxOrganization.description: The InfluxDB organization. Only necessary when using the http v2 api. Has no meaning when using v2 compatibility api.

influxToken.description: The InfluxDB access token. Only necessary when using the http v2 api. If the v2 compatibility api is used, use `user:password` instead.

influxVerify.description: Set to `false` to disable certificate verification for https endpoints. If not set, PVE default is `true`.

mtu.description: MTU (maximum transmission unit) for metrics transmission over UDP. If not set, PVE default is `1500` (allowed `512` - `65536`).

name.description: Unique name that will be ID of this metric server in PVE.

opentelemetryCompression.description: OpenTelemetry compression algorithm for requests. Choice is between `none` | `gzip`. If not set, PVE default is `gzip`.

opentelemetryHeaders.description: OpenTelemetry custom HTTP headers as JSON, base64 encoded.

opentelemetryMaxBodySize.description: OpenTelemetry maximum request body size in bytes. If not set, PVE default is `10000000`.

opentelemetryProto.description: Protocol for OpenTelemetry. Choice is between `http` | `https`. If not set, PVE default is `https`.

opentelemetryResourceAttributes.description: OpenTelemetry additional resource attributes as JSON, base64 encoded.

opentelemetryTimeout.description: OpenTelemetry HTTP request timeout in seconds. If not set, PVE default is `5`.

opentelemetryVerifySsl.description: OpenTelemetry verify SSL certificates. If not set, PVE default is `true`.

server.description: Server dns name or IP address.

timeout.description: TCP socket timeout in seconds. If not set, PVE default is `1`.

type.description: Plugin type. Choice is between `graphite` | `influxdb` | `opentelemetry`.

disable.description: Set this to `true` to disable this metric server. Defaults to `false`.

graphiteProto.description: Protocol to send graphite data. Choice is between `udp` | `tcp`. If not set, PVE default is `udp`.

influxApiPathPrefix.description: An API path prefix inserted between `<host>:<port>/` and `/api2/`. Can be useful if the InfluxDB service runs behind a reverse proxy.

influxBucket.description: The InfluxDB bucket/db. Only necessary when using the http v2 api.

influxDbProto.description: Protocol for InfluxDB. Choice is between `udp` | `http` | `https`. If not set, PVE default is `udp`.

influxMaxBodySize.description: InfluxDB max-body-size in bytes. Requests are batched up to this size. If not set, PVE default is `25000000`.

influxOrganization.description: The InfluxDB organization. Only necessary when using the http v2 api. Has no meaning when using v2 compatibility api.

influxToken.description: The InfluxDB access token. Only necessary when using the http v2 api. If the v2 compatibility api is used, use `user:password` instead.

influxVerify.description: Set to `false` to disable certificate verification for https endpoints. If not set, PVE default is `true`.

mtu.description: MTU (maximum transmission unit) for metrics transmission over UDP. If not set, PVE default is `1500` (allowed `512` - `65536`).

name.description: Unique name that will be ID of this metric server in PVE.

opentelemetryCompression.description: OpenTelemetry compression algorithm for requests. Choice is between `none` | `gzip`. If not set, PVE default is `gzip`.

opentelemetryHeaders.description: OpenTelemetry custom HTTP headers as JSON, base64 encoded.

proxmoxve:network/linux/bond:Bond.description: Manages a Linux Bond network interface in a Proxmox VE node.

autostart.description: Automatically start interface on boot (defaults to `true`).

bondMode.description: The bonding mode. Possible values are `balance-rr`, `active-backup`, `balance-xor`, `broadcast`, `802.3ad`, `balance-tlb`, `balance-alb`.

bondPrimary.description: The primary interface for `active-backup` bond mode. Specifies which slave interface should be the active one.

bondXmitHashPolicy.description: The transmit hash policy for `balance-xor` and `802.3ad` bond modes. Possible values are `layer2`, `layer2+3`, `layer3+4`.

mtu.description: The interface MTU.

name.description: The interface name. Must be `bond[N]`, where 0 ≤ N (e.g. bond0, bond1), or any alphanumeric string that starts with a character and is at most 10 characters long.

timeoutReload.description: Timeout for network reload operations in seconds (defaults to `100`).

bondPrimary.description: The primary interface for `active-backup` bond mode. Specifies which slave interface should be the active one.

bondXmitHashPolicy.description: The transmit hash policy for `balance-xor` and `802.3ad` bond modes. Possible values are `layer2`, `layer2+3`, `layer3+4`.

mtu.description: The interface MTU.

name.description: The interface name. Must be `bond[N]`, where 0 ≤ N (e.g. bond0, bond1), or any alphanumeric string that starts with a character and is at most 10 characters long.

enabled.description: Enable host firewall rules (defaults to `true`).

logLevelForward.description: Log level for forwarded traffic. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

logLevelIn.description: Log level for incoming traffic. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

logLevelOut.description: Log level for outgoing traffic. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

ndp.description: Enable NDP - Neighbor Discovery Protocol (defaults to `true`).

nfConntrackMax.description: Maximum number of tracked connections (defaults to `262144`). Minimum value is `32768`.

nfConntrackTcpTimeoutEstablished.description: Conntrack established timeout in seconds (defaults to `432000` - 5 days). Minimum value is `7875`.

nftables.description: Enable nftables based firewall (tech preview, defaults to `false`).

nosmurfs.description: Enable SMURFS filter (defaults to `true`).

smurfLogLevel.description: Log level for SMURFS filter. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

tcpFlagsLogLevel.description: Log level for illegal tcp flags filter. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

logLevelOut.description: Log level for outgoing traffic. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

ndp.description: Enable NDP - Neighbor Discovery Protocol (defaults to `true`).

nfConntrackMax.description: Maximum number of tracked connections (defaults to `262144`). Minimum value is `32768`.

nfConntrackTcpTimeoutEstablished.description: Conntrack established timeout in seconds (defaults to `432000` - 5 days). Minimum value is `7875`.

nftables.description: Enable nftables based firewall (tech preview, defaults to `false`).

nosmurfs.description: Enable SMURFS filter (defaults to `true`).

smurfLogLevel.description: Log level for SMURFS filter. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

tcpFlagsLogLevel.description: Log level for illegal tcp flags filter. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering FirewallLegacy resources.

enabled.description: Enable host firewall rules (defaults to `true`).

logLevelIn.description: Log level for incoming traffic. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

logLevelOut.description: Log level for outgoing traffic. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

nfConntrackTcpTimeoutEstablished.description: Conntrack established timeout in seconds (defaults to `432000` - 5 days). Minimum value is `7875`.

nftables.description: Enable nftables based firewall (tech preview, defaults to `false`).

nosmurfs.description: Enable SMURFS filter (defaults to `true`).

smurfLogLevel.description: Log level for SMURFS filter. Must be one of: `emerg`, `alert`, `crit`, `err`, `warning`, `notice`, `info`, `debug`, `nolog` (defaults to `nolog`).

datastoreId.description: The identifier for the target datastore.

fileName.description: The file name for the pulled OCI image. If not provided, it will be generated automatically. The file will be stored as a .tar file.

overwrite.description: By default `true`. If `true` and the OCI image size has changed in the datastore, it will be replaced. If `false`, there will be no check.

overwriteUnmanaged.description: If `true` and an OCI image with the same name already exists in the datastore, it will be deleted and the new image will be pulled. If `false` and the image already exists, an error will be returned.

reference.description: The reference to the OCI image.

uploadTimeout.description: The OCI image pull timeout in seconds. Default is 600 (10min).

queryUserinfo.description: Query the OpenID userinfo endpoint for claims. Required when the identity provider does not include claims in the ID token.

scopes.description: Space-separated list of OpenID scopes to request.

usernameClaim.description: OpenID claim used to generate the unique username. Common values are `subject`, `username`, `email`, and `upn`.

proxmoxve:sdn/zone/evpn:Evpn.description:
EVPN Zone in Proxmox SDN. The EVPN zone creates a routable Layer 3 network, capable of spanning across multiple clusters.

## Example Usage

<!--Start PulumiCodeChooser -->
```typescript
import * as pulumi from "@pulumi/pulumi";
import * as proxmoxve from "@muhlba91/pulumi-proxmoxve";

const example = new proxmoxve.sdn.zone.Evpn("example", {
    resourceId: "evpn1",
    nodes: ["pve"],
    controller: "evpn-controller1",
    vrfVxlan: 4000,
    advertiseSubnets: true,
    disableArpNdSuppression: false,
    exitNodes: [
        "pve-exit1",
        "pve-exit2",
    ],
    exitNodesLocalRouting: true,
    primaryExitNode: "pve-exit1",
    rtImport: "65000:65000",
    mtu: 1450,
    dns: "1.1.1.1",
    dnsZone: "example.com",
    ipam: "pve",
    reverseDns: "1.1.1.1",
});
```


dnsZone.description: DNS domain name. Used to register hostnames, such as `<hostname>.<domain>`. The DNS zone must already exist on the DNS server.

exitNodes.description: List of exit nodes for EVPN.

exitNodesLocalRouting.description: Enable local routing for EVPN exit nodes.

mtu.description: MTU value for the zone. There is no support to reset this value back to PVE default once set due to API limitation.

nodes.description: The Proxmox nodes which the zone and associated VNets should be deployed on

pending.description: Indicates if the zone has pending configuration changes that need to be applied.

resourceId.description: The unique identifier of the SDN zone.

state.description: Indicates the current state of the zone.

vrfVxlan.description: VRF VXLAN-ID used for dedicated routing interconnect between VNets. It must be different than the VXLAN-ID of the VNets.
